text: Handling deadlines well is an important skill in both work and study. Good time management helps you break large projects into smaller tasks and set clear milestones. Prioritization is also key; you should identify the most urgent or important work first and do it early in your schedule. Scope management means knowing your limits—avoid taking on too much, and be realistic about what you can finish in the given time. Finally, clear communication with your team or manager is essential. If you see a problem, raise it early, and ask for help if needed. These strategies reduce stress and help you deliver quality work on time.
